name: MoneyGram
description_short: MoneyGram San Carlos, California — MoneyGram operates a network of physical locations offering money transfer, check cashing, and bill payment services ...
description_long:
MoneyGram is a money transfer service operating through a distributed network of physical agent locations rather than traditional brick-and-mortar branches. The company partners with retail establishments like CVS/Longs to provide financial services to consumers who prefer or require in-person transactions. Their primary business model relies on third-party retail partnerships to deliver services in local communities.

MoneyGram's core offerings include domestic and international money transfers, check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, and bill payment services. Customers can initiate transactions both online through their website and offline at physical partner locations. The service emphasizes accessibility and convenience by allowing customers to search for nearby locations and manage transfers through multiple channels.

The company distinguishes itself through its extensive network of agent locations embedded in familiar retail environments like pharmacies, making services geographically accessible across numerous communities. This retail partnership model contrasts with traditional financial institutions by eliminating the need for dedicated branches while maintaining physical service points for customers who need them.

MoneyGram serves primarily unbanked and underbanked populations who lack access to traditional banking services or prefer cash-based transactions. However, the service model inherently involves higher transaction fees typical of money transfer services, and availability depends entirely on partner location hours and participation. The company does not offer deposit accounts, credit products, or investment services.
address: 1324 San Carlos Ave, San Carlos, CA 94070

company_info.headquarters: 1324 San Carlos Ave, San Carlos, CA 94070

pros: Extensive retail location network including accessible partner sites like CVS/Longs pharmacies
Both online and offline transaction capabilities for money transfers
Domestic and international money transfer options
Multiple service types available at single locations including checks, money orders, wire transfers, and bill payments
Searchable location finder tool with real-time hours and address information
Services available during extended retail hours (example location open until 11:59 PM)
cons: No physical branded locations; entirely dependent on third-party retail partners
Limited to services offered through partner agreements; cannot guarantee availability of all services at every location
Typical money transfer fees apply, making services more expensive than traditional banking for frequent users
No deposit accounts, savings products, or credit facilities offered
Service availability restricted to partner location operating hours
best_for: Unbanked and underbanked consumers without access to traditional banking
Individuals needing occasional money transfers or check cashing services
Customers requiring in-person financial transactions at convenient retail locations
Those sending or receiving international remittances through established networks
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for unbanked consumers and those needing occasional check cashing or money transfer services without traditional bank accounts. The primary caveat is that service quality and availability depend on partner location participation and hours, and transaction costs are substantially higher than traditional banking alternatives.
services: Agent location services through retail partnerships
